summary: NVIDIA posted a massive Q1 beat after the close - revenue $81.62B vs estimate $79.19B, adj EPS $1.87, Q2 guidance $89-92.8B, and an $80B additional buyback; the AI infrastructure trade remains firmly intact despite an initial -1.5% reaction that was immediately bought.

The April FOMC minutes were unexpectedly hawkish: a majority of officials signaled rate hikes may be warranted if inflation persists above 2%, sending 30-year Treasury yields toward 5.5% (last seen in 2004) and forcing markets to price hiking risk rather than cuts.

Iran War dynamics dominated macro - Trump's 'final stages' peace deal comments briefly sent oil below $97, US Marines boarded and released an Iranian tanker, but a tense Trump-Netanyahu call revealed Israel wants a military green light before any deal - real uncertainty about the timeline.

The Strait of Hormuz remains closed; fertilizer prices up 44% since the war began, US gasoline at $4.53/gallon, and crude inventory draws hitting record rates - with biancoresearch explicitly warning the consensus view that 'the Strait opens any day' is dangerously wrong.

In crypto, Zcash ($ZEC) surged ~34% and Hyperliquid ($HYPE) +27% on the week; two HYPE spot ETFs launched drawing $22.3M in early inflows, USDC formally integrated with Hyperliquid, and approximately 50% of Hyperliquid volume is now traditional assets (stocks, oil, S&P500, pre-IPO).

OpenAI is reportedly filing for IPO within days targeting a September listing, SpaceX perps imply a $2.5T valuation on Hyperliquid, and an internal OpenAI model autonomously disproved an Erdos math conjecture unsolved since 1946 - the AI breakthrough cycle is visibly accelerating.

Stagflation is quietly entering 2026 consensus: Bloomberg surveys show GDP forecasts revised down to 2.15% while CPI is revised up to 3.30%, bond markets globally are in what one observer called a 'slow-motion car wreck,' and 40% of fund managers now see a second wave of inflation as their top tail risk.
